description: Distributed systems - consensus, consistency, fault tolerance.

facts.context: The CAP theorem states distributed systems cannot simultaneously guarantee:
distractors: only any two of the three properties at once
performance and correctness together always
scalability under any network conditions

facts.context: Paxos consensus algorithm requires for progress:
distractors: all nodes to be online and reachable always
synchronous network with bounded message delay
exactly three nodes in the cluster minimum

facts.truth: causal ordering of events across nodes
distractors: exact wall-clock timestamps synchronized globally
only the count of messages between node pairs
CPU cycle counts for each operation performed

facts.context: Two-phase commit can block indefinitely when:
distractors: any participant votes no during commit phase
network latency exceeds a configured threshold
more than half of participants fail simultaneously

facts.context: Raft consensus uses randomized election timeouts to:
facts.truth: prevent split votes in leader election
distractors: ensure deterministic round-robin leader selection
always elect the node with highest identifier
delegate election to an external coordinator
all reads return the most recent write immediately
updates are never lost under any circumstances
consistency is achieved within bounded time always

facts.context: CRDTs (Conflict-free Replicated Data Types) achieve consistency by:
distractors: using locks to prevent concurrent modifications
requiring a central coordinator for all updates
rejecting conflicting updates automatically
broadcasting to all nodes simultaneously always
hierarchical propagation through leader nodes
deterministic ordering based on node identifiers
exact physical time measurements globally
partial ordering that can have concurrent events
ordering only within a single process boundary

facts.context: Byzantine fault tolerance requires at least:
facts.truth: 3f+1 nodes to tolerate f Byzantine failures
distractors: 2f+1 nodes like crash fault tolerance requires
f+1 nodes with majority voting for consensus
exactly f nodes designated as backup replicas

facts.truth: appear instantaneous at some point between invocation and response
distractors: complete in bounded time regardless of failures
execute in exactly the order they were submitted
be visible to all nodes at the same instant

facts.context: Distributed leases for coordination work by:
distractors: permanently assigning ownership to one node
using consensus for every single access request
allowing concurrent access with merge resolution
